chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمِنْبَرِ وَ الرَّوْضَةِ وَ مَقَامِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 217 – The Pulpit, and the garden, and place of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/265)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/265)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا فَرَغْتَ مِنَ الدُّعَاءِ عِنْدَ قَبْرِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَائْتِ الْمِنْبَرَ فَامْسَحْهُ بِيَدِكَ وَ خُذْ بِرُمَّانَتَيْهِ وَ هُمَا السُّفْلَاوَانِ وَ امْسَحْ عَيْنَيْكَ وَ وَجْهَكَ بِهِ فَإِنَّهُ يُقَالُ إِنَّهُ شِفَاءُ الْعَيْنِ

وَ قُمْ عِنْدَهُ فَاحْمَدِ اللَّهَ وَ أَثْنِ عَلَيْهِ وَ سَلْ حَاجَتَكَ فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ مَا بَيْنَ مِنْبَرِي وَ بَيْتِي رَوْضَةٌ مِنْ رِيَاضِ الْجَنَّةِ وَ مِنْبَرِي عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ الْجَنَّةِ وَ التُّرْعَةُ هِيَ الْبَابُ الصَّغِيرُ

ثُمَّ تَأْتِي مَقَامَ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَتُصَلِّي فِيهِ مَا بَدَا لَكَ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتَ الْمَسْجِدَ فَصَلِّ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ إِذَا خَرَجْتَ فَاصْنَعْ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ وَ أَكْثِرْ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ فِي مَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Ibn Abu Umeyr and Safwan Bin yahya, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When you are free from the supplication by the grave of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, then go to the Pulpit and wipe it with your hand, and grab its two pomegranates, and they are both ceramic, and wipe your eyes and your face with it, for it is said that it is a healing for the eyes.

And stand by it, so Praise Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and Extoll upon Him<sup>azwj</sup>, and ask your need, for Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘What is between my<sup>as</sup> Pulpit and my<sup>saww</sup> house, there is a garden from the Gardens of the Paradise, and my<sup>saww</sup> Pulpit is a channel from the channels of the Paradise, and the channel, it is the small door.

Then go to the place of the Prophetsaww, and Pray Salaat therein whatever come to you. So when you enter the Masjid, then send Blessings upon the Prophetsaww. And when you exit, so do similar to that, and frequent from the Praying of Salaat in the Masjid of the Rasool-Allahsaww’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and Muhammad ibn Isma‘il from af-Fadf ibn Shadhan from ibn abu ‘Umayr and Safwan ibn Yahya from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once said to me, ‘When you complete your prayer near the grave of the Holy Prophet, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, then go to the pulpit. You should touch it with your hand, hold the two pomegranates, which are made of ceramic materials and wipe your hand over your eyes and face. It is said that it is cure for the eyes. Then stand up near the pulpit, thank and praise Allah and ask for your wishes. The Messenger of Allah has said, “Between my pulpit and my house there is a garden of the gardens of paradise and my pulpit is a Tur‘ah (passage) of the passage of paradise.” Al-Tur‘a is a small door. Thereafter you should go to the station of the Holy Prophet, perform salat (prayer) as much as you like. When you enter the Masjid, then say, “O Allah, grant salawat (favors and compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause). When you leave do as you just had done. In the Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, perform as many salat (prayer) as you can.”
